name: clinical_trial_placebo_pediatric_oncology
premise: Dr. Naomi Weiss chairs an IRB meeting for a pediatric oncology trial comparing a novel kinase inhibitor to standard-of-care placebo during maintenance therapy when no proven superior exists yet overseas. Parent advocate Chris Ortega demands all children receive the experimental arm citing compassionate-use stories. Regulators require placebo control for approval; without it, the drug may never reach rural hospitals. Enrollment closes tomorrow; half the slots are unfilled because families fear randomization.

agents.name: Dr. Naomi Weiss
agents.identity: I am the IRB chair responsible for protecting child participants while enabling scientifically valid research that could help future patients.
agents.goal: I want equipoise honored without crushing hope for families who misunderstand placebo arms in cancer maintenance.

agents.name: Chris Ortega
agents.identity: I am a parent advocate whose child aged out of prior trials and who organizes support groups for families navigating randomization anxiety.
agents.goal: I want every child offered the investigational drug when toxicity profiles look favorable and overseas peers already access expanded programs.

choices: I will approve the trial as written with enhanced consent counseling and post-trial access commitments, accepting that some enrolled children will receive placebo during maintenance.
I will suspend local enrollment and petition the sponsor for a single-arm compassionate cohort alongside a smaller controlled substudy, accepting months of delay for all patients here.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Sponsor representatives are waiting for IRB signatures before flights tonight. Dr. Naomi Weiss must choose between proceeding with placebo controls or delaying for redesign. What does Dr. Naomi Weiss do?
